# Dubai city guide

## Overview

Dubai is what happens when ambition has no ceiling. Record-breaking skyscrapers, artificial islands, indoor ski slopes in the desert, and malls the size of small cities. But beyond the flash, there's a genuine cultural layer in the historic Creek district and excellent food from every corner of the globe. Combine with a day trip to Abu Dhabi for the Louvre and Sheikh Zayed Mosque.

**Best time to visit**: Nov-Mar for pleasant weather (20-25C). Avoid Jun-Sep when temperatures hit 45C+.
**Currency**: UAE Dirham (AED). 3.67 AED ≈ 1 USD (fixed peg).
**Language tip**: Arabic is official but English is spoken everywhere. "Shukran" (thank you) and "Habibi" (friend/dear) go a long way.

## Neighborhoods

| Neighborhood | Vibe | Best for | Transit |
|---|---|---|---|
| Downtown Dubai | Iconic, modern | Burj Khalifa, Dubai Mall, fountains | Metro Red Line, Burj Khalifa/Dubai Mall Station |
| Dubai Marina | Glitzy, waterfront | Beach walks, dining, yacht views | Metro Red Line, DMCC Station |
| Old Dubai/Deira | Historic, authentic | Gold Souk, Spice Souk, creek crossings | Metro Green Line, Al Ras Station |
| Bur Dubai | Cultural, budget | Dubai Museum, textile souk, Al Fahidi | Metro Green Line, Al Fahidi Station |
| JBR (Jumeirah Beach Residence) | Beach, casual | Beach, The Walk, Ain Dubai | Tram, JBR Station |
| DIFC | Business, upscale | Fine dining, galleries, cocktail bars | Metro Red Line, Financial Centre Station |
| Al Quoz | Industrial, artsy | Alserkal Avenue galleries, coffee roasters | Taxi from Business Bay |
| Palm Jumeirah | Resort, luxurious | Atlantis, beach clubs, monorail views | Palm Monorail from Nakheel Station |

## Food

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description | Must-order |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Al Ustad Special Kabab | Middle Eastern | Bur Dubai | $ | A tiny Iranian kabab shop open since 1978. Simple, honest, and the best kababs in Dubai. | Chicken kabab, kubideh, fresh bread |
| Ravi Restaurant | Pakistani | Satwa | $ | The most famous budget restaurant in Dubai. Packed at midnight with everyone from laborers to Lamborghini drivers. | Butter chicken, nihari, fresh naan |
| Pierchic | Seafood | Al Qasr | $$$ | Fine dining on a pier extending into the Arabian Gulf. Sunset views, fresh catch, romantic setting. | Seafood platter, lobster |
| Al Mallah | Shawarma | Al Dhiyafah St | $ | The shawarma spot Dubai residents swear by. Freshly grilled, perfectly wrapped, served fast. | Chicken shawarma, falafel wrap, fresh juice |
| Zuma | Japanese | DIFC | $$$ | Contemporary Japanese cuisine in a stunning DIFC setting. Power lunch destination, vibrant brunch on weekends. | Black cod miso, robata meats, brunch |
| Bu Qtair | Seafood | Umm Suqeim | $ | A former beach shack (now in a building) serving fried fish and shrimp with rice. Cash only, no menu. | Fried hammour, shrimp, rice |
| Logma | Emirati | Multiple | $$ | Modern Emirati food with a twist. One of the few places to try local cuisine done right. | Chebab (Emirati pancakes), lamb machboos, luqaimat |
| Operation Falafel | Middle Eastern | Multiple | $ | Fresh, customizable falafel wraps with creative fillings. Quick, healthy, and satisfying. | Falafel wrap with halloumi, mango iced tea |
| La Petite Maison | French | DIFC | $$$ | Riviera-style French cuisine. The warm prawns and truffle pizza are Dubai power-lunch staples. | Warm prawns, truffle pizza |
| 3Fils | Asian fusion | Jumeirah Fishing Harbour | $$ | Tiny waterfront spot with creative sushi and Asian bites. Reservations essential, no-frills, flavor-focused. | Wagyu sando, salmon rice, prawn tacos |

## Landmarks and activities

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Time needed | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Burj Khalifa | Views | Downtown | $$-$$$ | 1-2 hours | The world's tallest building at 828 meters. Book "At the Top" tickets online. Sunset timing is magical. |
| Dubai Mall | Shopping | Downtown | Free | 3-4 hours | Over 1,200 shops plus an aquarium, ice rink, and dinosaur skeleton. The Dubai Fountain show outside is free every 30 minutes. |
| Dubai Creek and Abra Ride | Culture | Deira/Bur Dubai | $ | 1-2 hours | Take a traditional wooden abra boat across the creek for 1 AED. Connects the Gold Souk to the Textile Souk. |
| Gold Souk | Shopping | Deira | Free | 1 hour | Hundreds of shops selling gold jewelry. Window shopping is free, bargaining is expected if buying. |
| Desert Safari | Adventure | Outside Dubai | $$-$$$ | Half day | Dune bashing, camel rides, BBQ dinner under the stars. Every hotel can arrange it. Go for the sunset. |
| Alserkal Avenue | Art | Al Quoz | Free | 2-3 hours | Dubai's art district in converted warehouses. Galleries, coffee shops, indie cinema. Unexpected and cool. |
| Ain Dubai (Dubai Eye) | Views | JBR | $$$ | 1 hour | The world's largest observation wheel. 250 meters high with views of Palm Jumeirah and the skyline. |
| Museum of the Future | Culture | Trade Centre | $$ | 2 hours | The stunning torus-shaped building is an attraction itself. Inside, immersive exhibits about future technologies. |
| Palm Jumeirah Boardwalk | Walk | Palm | Free | 1-2 hours | Walk along the crescent for views of the Atlantis and the Dubai skyline. Best at sunset. |
| Abu Dhabi Day Trip | Day trip | Abu Dhabi | $$-$$$ | Full day | Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque (free, stunning), Louvre Abu Dhabi, and Corniche Beach. 1.5 hours by car from Dubai. |

## Nightlife

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| WHITE Dubai | Club | Meydan | $$$ | Rooftop mega-club with international DJs. Thursday and Friday nights are the big ones. |
| Barasti Beach Bar | Beach bar | Le Meridien | $$ | Sand-between-your-toes beach bar. Casual, fun, good for afternoon drinks into the evening. |
| Galaxy Bar | Lounge | DIFC | $$$ | Sleek cocktail bar atop a tower. Galaxy-themed cocktails with Dubai skyline views. |
| Lock, Stock and Barrel | Pub | JBR/Multiple | $$ | Lively sports bar and pub. Craft beers, live music, good burgers. The relaxed alternative to clubs. |
| Soho Garden | Club/lounge | Meydan | $$-$$$ | Multi-venue complex with different vibes, from pool parties to underground techno. |

## Transit tips

- **Metro**: Red and Green lines cover major attractions. Clean, modern, air-conditioned. Gold class available for a premium.
- **Tram**: Connects Dubai Marina, JBR, and links to the Metro. Useful for the beach area.
- **Nol card**: Rechargeable transit card for Metro, tram, and buses. Buy at any station.
- **Taxi**: Relatively cheap. RTA taxis are metered, safe, and air-conditioned. Use the Careem or Uber app.
- **Walking**: Only practical within individual developments (Marina Walk, Downtown). Dubai is a driving city.
- **Pro tip**: Friday brunch is a Dubai institution. Restaurants offer all-you-can-eat/drink packages. Book ahead for popular spots.
- **Abu Dhabi**: 1.5 hours by car or bus. E101 bus from Ibn Battuta is cheap. Rent a car for flexibility.

## Useful Arabic phrases

| English | Arabic | Romanization |
|---|---|---|
| Hello | مرحبا | marhaba |
| Thank you | شكرا | shukran |
| Yes | نعم | na'am |
| No | لا | la |
| How much? | بكم؟ | bikam? |
| God willing | إن شاء الله | inshallah |
| Beautiful | ماشاء الله | mashallah |
| Goodbye | مع السلامة | ma'a salama |
